POL 202.name: Advanced Polish through Contemporary Culture
POL 202.prerequisites: POL 104; or appropriate score on the department placement test.
POL 202.description: Development of skills in spoken and written Polish, within the context of contemporary Polish culture, via a variety of sources, such as: news articles, comics, music.Course Information: Prerequisite(s): POL 104; or appropriate score on the department placement test.

POL 203.name: Advanced Polish through Short Stories
POL 203.prerequisites: POL 104; or appropriate score on the department placement test.
POL 203.description: Development of advanced language skills: writing, reading, listening comprehension, and speaking. Exploration and analysis of Polish cultural tropes within the context of short stories. Course Information: May be repeated for a maximum of 6 hours. May be repeated if topics vary. Prerequisite(s): POL 104; or appropriate score on the department placement test.

POL 204.name: Advanced Polish in Healthcare Settings
POL 204.prerequisites: Grade of C or better in POL 104; and appropriate score on the department placement test.
POL 204.description: Develops students’ linguistic competence in Polish required in healthcare settings through introduction of specialized medical terminology and related communicative skills. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): Grade of C or better in POL 104; and appropriate score on the department placement test.

POL 220.name: Modern Polish-Jewish Culture and Literatures
POL 220.prerequisites: ENGL 160 or ENGL 161. UIC GE Creative Arts course, and UIC GE World Cultures course.
POL 220.description: Polish-Jewish literature and non-fiction works in English translation from Polish, Yiddish and Hebrew are studied as expressions of a distinct Ashkenazi or East European Jewish culture that developed on historically Polish lands. Course Information: Same as JST 220 and CEES 220. Prerequisite(s): ENGL 160 or ENGL 161. UIC GE Creative Arts course, and UIC GE World Cultures course.

POL 234.name: The Making of Modern Poland
POL 234.prerequisites: ENGL 160 or completion of any 100-level history course. UIC GE Individual and Society course, and UIC GE Past course.
POL 234.description: Exploration of political, socioeconomic, and cultural developments since the first Polish state, the union with Lithuania, the struggle for independence, and Communist rule to the present. Course Information: Same as HIST 234. Prerequisite(s):ENGL 160 or completion of any 100-level history course. UIC GE Individual and Society course, and UIC GE Past course.

POL 242.name: Polish Poetry for Beginners
POL 242.description: Polish poetry from various literary epochs read as an introduction to Polish literature and explained against the basic historical, cultural, and transcultural contexts. Course Information: Taught in English.

POL 321.name: Introduction to Polish Literature
POL 321.prerequisites: Junior standing or consent of the instructor.
POL 321.description: Old Polish literature from medieval Latin and vernacular texts to masterpieces of the Polish Enlightenment. Course Information: Taught in English. Prerequisite(s): Junior standing or consent of the instructor.

POL 399.name: Independent Study
POL 399.prerequisites: Junior standing, consent of the instructor and consent of the head of the department. Class Schedule Information: This course counts toward the limited number of independent study hours accepted toward the degree and the major.
POL 399.description: Investigation of special problems under the general direction of a staff member. Course Information: May be repeated to a maximum of 6 hours. Prerequisite(s): Junior standing, consent of the instructor and consent of the head of the department. Class Schedule Information: This course counts toward the limited number of independent study hours accepted toward the degree and the major.

POL 460.name: Studies in Polish Literature
POL 460.prerequisites: Credit or concurrent registration in CEES 224 or Credit or concurrent registration in CEES 225; or consent of the instructor.
POL 460.description: Major literary Polish writers recognized in the world, translated extensively into English; their poetics, worldviews as compared with foreign writers; historical and philosophical background. Topics may vary. Course Information: 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. May be repeated to a maximum of 9 hours for undergraduates, or 12 hours for graduate students, if topics vary. Only 6 hours may be applied toward the undergraduate major in Polish. Taught in English. Polish majors will be required to complete some assignments in Polish. Prerequisite(s): Credit or concurrent registration in CEES 224 or Credit or concurrent registration in CEES 225; or consent of the instructor.

POL 499.name: Independent Study
POL 499.prerequisites: Senior or graduate standing, consent of the instructor and consent of the head of the department. Class Schedule Information: This course counts toward the limited number of independent study hours accepted toward the undergraduate degree and the major.
POL 499.description: Investigation of special problems under the general direction of a staff member. Course Information: May be repeated to a maximum of 8 hours. Graduate students may register for more than one section per term. Prerequisite(s): Senior or graduate standing, consent of the instructor and consent of the head of the department. Class Schedule Information: This course counts toward the limited number of independent study hours accepted toward the undergraduate degree and the major.

POLS 101.name: Introduction to American Government and Politics
POLS 101.description: Introduction to American political ideas, individual and group political behavior, institutions of national government, and public policy. Course Information: May be taught in blended learning format. Please check the online schedule of classes for blended sections. Class Schedule Information:  To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Discussion/Recitation and one Lecture-Discussion. IAI GECC Soc/Behav Sci course, and UIC GE Individual and Society course, and UIC GE US Society course.

POLS 112.name: Black Politics and Culture in the United States
POLS 112.description: The shifting relationship between black politics and black cultural forms across various historical periods. Course Information: Same as BLST 103. UIC GE Individual and Society course, and UIC GE US Society course.

POLS 120.name: Introduction to Political Theory
POLS 120.description: Competing accounts of the relationships among individuals, society, and the state. Analysis of differing conceptions of human nature through readings in ancient and modern classics. Class Schedule Information:  To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Discussion/Recitation and one Lecture-Discussion. IAI Major PLS course, and UIC GE Individual and Society course, and UIC GE Past course.

POLS 130.name: Introduction to Comparative Politics
POLS 130.description: Comparative study of political institutions, political culture, and political processes in selected major countries of the world. Course Information: Same as INST 130. Some sections may be offered fully online or in a blended-online and classroom course setting. Consult the Schedule of Classes for the relevant semester to determine the mode of instruction. Use of computer and internet access is required for all online and blended sections. A high-speed connection, while not required, is strongly suggested. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Discussion/Recitation and one Lecture-Discussion. IAI GECC Soc/Behav Sci course, and UIC GE Individual and Society course.

POLS 184.name: Introduction to International Relations
POLS 184.description: Political, military, and economic relations between states, international organizations and transnational actors. Problems of war, imperialism and the world economy. Prospects for global cooperation. Course Information: Same as INST 184. Extensive computer use required. Some sections may be offered fully online or in a blended-online and classroom course setting. Consult the Schedule of Classes for the relevant semester to determine the mode of instruction. Use of computer and internet access is required for all online and blended sections. A high-speed connection, while not required, is strongly suggested. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Discussion/Recitation and one Lecture-Discussion. IAI GECC Soc/Behav Sci course, and UIC GE Individual and Society course, and UIC GE World Cultures course.

POLS 195.name: Politics of Science and Technology
POLS 195.description: The interrelationships among science, technology, and politics in the US. Political and social problems caused by technological change, polarization of science and technology, how social media changes, government patronage of science and technology. Course Information: Some sections may be offered fully online or in a blended-online and classroom course setting. Consult the Schedule of Classes for the relevant semester to determine the mode of instruction. Use of computer and internet access is required for all online and blended sections. A high-speed connection, while not required, is strongly suggested. UIC GE US Society course.

POLS 200.name: Methods of Political Science
POLS 200.description: Methods of political science. Conducting primary research projects using the methods most frequently employed by political scientists.

POLS 201.name: Political Data Analysis
POLS 201.prerequisites: MATH 090; or MATH 105; or MATH 108; or consent of the instructor.
POLS 201.description: Introduction to basic elements of statistics and data analysis for political science. Includes descriptive and inferential statistics; introduction to UIC computer facility and statistics software. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): MATH 090; or MATH 105; or MATH 108; or consent of the instructor.

POLS 202.name: Topics in Political Practice
POLS 202.description: Selected topics and issues in Political Science. Topics vary across the subfields of Political Science. Course Information: May be repeated to a maximum of 6 hours if topics vary. Students may register in more than one section per term.

POLS 203.name: Popular Culture and Politics
POLS 203.description: Use of popular culture and its products in the analysis of contemporary political themes through the lens of political processes, state and legal institutions, political behavior, and the exercise of power. Topics may vary. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 101.

POLS 204.name: Democracy and Its Discontents
POLS 204.description: Explores the challenges past and present democracies face. Topics include democratic erosion, weak institutions, polarization, populism, corruption, and violence and democracy. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 130.

POLS 206.name: Political Behavior
POLS 206.description: An introduction to political behavior; includes the structure and functions of political attitudes, the role of personality, political socialization, electoral behavior, and related topics. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 101.

POLS 207.name: The Mass Media and Politics
POLS 207.prerequisites: One course in political science, sociology or contemporary history.
POLS 207.description: Impact of mass media coverage on political attitudes and the conduct of American politics. Communication policies and media institutions in times of rapid technological change. Course Information: Same as COMM 207. Some sections may be offered fully online or in a blended-online and classroom course setting. Consult the Schedule of Classes for the mode of instruction. Use of computer and internet access is required for all online and blended sections. A high speed connection, while not required, is strongly suggested. Prerequisite(s): One course in political science, sociology or contemporary history.

POLS 209.name: Latinos and Politics
POLS 209.description: Latino politics and politicians in the context of the American political system. The political system, Latino participation, experience, and research on political processes. Course Information: Same as LALS 283.  Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture and one Discussion. UIC GE US Society course.

POLS 210.name: Introduction to Urban Politics
POLS 210.description: Growth and legal problems of cities: intergovernmental relations; powers and forms of government; pressure group activity; municipal functions and services; globalization and global cities; and revenue issues. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 101.

POLS 211.name: Chicago's Future
POLS 211.description: Emphasis on the political and governmental future of Chicago. Covers racial and ethnic politics; metropolitan, city, and neighborhood government; machine versus reform politics; and Chicago as a global city. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 101.

POLS 212.name: State Government
POLS 212.description: Organization and powers of state governments in the U.S.; constitutions and problems of revision; the major institutions and their interrelationships, intergovernmental relations. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 101.

POLS 220.name: Mock Trial I
POLS 220.description: Introduction to the concept of mock trial. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): Consent of the instructor.

POLS 221.name: Mock Trial II
POLS 221.description: Participation in mock trials. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): Consent of the instructor.

POLS 225.name: Lobbying, Advocacy, and Social Movements
POLS 225.description: The logic and power of collective action in democracies. Modes of state-societal relations: competition among societal actors or guided coordination between the state and societal actors. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 101.

POLS 226.name: Political Parties in Modern Democracies
POLS 226.description: Organization, role, and function of political parties in democratic settings. Types of party systems and ideological families. Link between elections and party systems. Opportunities for reform of parties and party systems. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 101 or POLS 130 or INST 130.

POLS 227.name: Voting Behavior and Elections
POLS 227.description: Examination of elections, voting behavior, election law, and voting theories and influences. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 101.

POLS 228.name: U
POLS 228.description: Introduction to the structures and processes of Congressional politics. Emphasis on elections, organization, interinstitutional relations and authorizations and appropriations processes. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 101.

POLS 229.name: Executive Powers and the United States Presidency
POLS 229.description: Examination of such phenomena as presidential elections; the presidency and the Constitution; the presidency and public administration; the president as policy-maker; presidential  leadership. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 101.

POLS 231.name: Politics and Society of China
POLS 231.description: Main issues of contemporary Chinese politics and social change; the decline of the last imperial dynasty; the republican era; the rise of communism and China under the rule of Mao Zedong; and the reform period (post-1978). Course Information: Same as GLAS 231 and HIST 231. Recommended background: POLS 130 or INST 130. UIC GE World Cultures course.

POLS 232.name: Politics and Government of Korea
POLS 232.description:
Sources, dynamics, and patterns of politics in the two Koreas. Comparison of North and South Korea. Course Information: Same as GLAS 232.
Recommended background: POLS 130 or INST 130. UIC GE World Cultures course.

POLS 234.name: Politics and Government of Western Europe
POLS 234.description: Comparison of government and politics of major Western European countries. Topics include political culture, political parties, elections, legislatures, executive arrangements, and the European Union. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 130 or INST 130.

POLS 236.name: Politics and Government of Eastern Europe
POLS 236.description: Similarities and differences of the political system of eastern European states and the sources and meaning of political change. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 130 or INST 130.

POLS 242.name: Government and Politics of Latin America
POLS 242.description: An examination of government and politics in selected Latin American countries. Comparative and historical analysis of dictatorship, democracies, political institutions, and parties. Course Information: Same as LALS 242.

POLS 243.name: Politics and Government of the Middle East
POLS 243.description: Contemporary Middle East political institutions, culture, processes, and conflicts. Emphasis on interaction of traditional and modern forces, such as Islam, nationalism, political elites, ideologies, states. Course Information: Same as JST 243. Recommended Background: POLS 130 or INST 130. UIC GE World Cultures course.

POLS 246.name: History of American Capitalism
POLS 246.prerequisites: ENGL 161; or consent of the instructor. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture-Discussion and one Discussion. UIC GE Past course, and UIC GE US Society course.
POLS 246.description: Survey of the political, economic, cultural, and social history of capitalism in colonial British America and the United States, with special attention to property, labor, finance, public policy, ideology, and class relations. Course Information: Same as HIST 246. Prerequisite(s): ENGL 161; or consent of the instructor. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture-Discussion and one Discussion. UIC GE Past course, and UIC GE US Society course.

POLS 258.name: The Judicial Process
POLS 258.description: Dispute settlement and policy making in state and federal court systems; judicial decision making and the impact of decisions on the polity. Course Information: Some sections may be offered fully online or in a blended-online and classroom course setting. Consult the Schedule of Classes for the relevant semester to determine the mode of instruction. Use of computer and internet access is required for all online and blended sections. A high-speed connection, while not required, is strongly suggested. Recommended background: POLS 101.

POLS 259.name: Constitutional Conversations: Contemporary Debates in American Constitutional Law and Politics
POLS 259.prerequisites: POLS 101; or consent of the instructor.
POLS 259.description: Exploration of contemporary debates about the US Constitution, regarding foundational doctrines: limited government, republicanism, checks and balances, federalism, separation of powers, popular sovereignty, individual liberty, and equal protection. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): POLS 101; or consent of the instructor.

POLS 267.name: American Intellectual History to 1865
POLS 267.prerequisites: ENGL 161. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture and one Discussion. UIC GE Past course, and UIC GE US Society course.
POLS 267.description: Major issues in American intellectual history from the contacts of culture begun by European exploration on the land now known as the United States to the end of the American Civil War in 1865. Course Information: Same as HIST 267. Prerequisite(s): ENGL 161. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture and one Discussion. UIC GE Past course, and UIC GE US Society course.

POLS 268.name: American Intellectual History since 1865
POLS 268.prerequisites: ENGL 161. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture and one Discussion. UIC GE Past course, and UIC GE US Society course.
POLS 268.description: Major issues in American intellectual history and the history of ideas from the end of the Civil War to the present. Course Information: Same as HIST 268. Prerequisite(s): ENGL 161. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture and one Discussion. UIC GE Past course, and UIC GE US Society course.

POLS 274.name: The Politics of Blackness in Latin America
POLS 274.description: Examines race, culture, and politics in Latin America from historical and contemporary perspectives with a particular emphasis on the latter half of the 20th century and the beginning of the 21st century. Course Information: Same as BLST 274 and LALS 274. Recommended background: Three credit hours in BLST or LALS or POLS.

POLS 275.name: Gender in Latin America
POLS 275.description: Latin American women in historical perspective from pre-Columbian and Iberian societies to the present. Course Information: Same as GWS 275 and LALS 275. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture and one Discussion/Recitation. UIC GE Past course, and UIC GE World Cultures course.

POLS 281.name: United States Foreign Policy
POLS 281.description: Internal and external factors that influence formulation and execution of U.S. foreign policy. Major problems of contemporary foreign policy: constitutional, organizational, and intellectual factors. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 101.

POLS 282.name: Simulation on Political Organizations
POLS 282.description: Theoretical and practical approaches, preparing students for inter-collegiate competitions, role-playing delegates in state, national, international, or legal organizations. Topics vary and include Model United Nations, Model European Union. Course Information: May be repeated to a maximum of 6 hours with approval of the department. Prerequisite(s): Consent of the instructor.

POLS 283.name: International Political Economy
POLS 283.description: Political underpinnings and ramifications of international economic relations. Foreign trade policy, multinational corporations, oil North-South relations, economic warfare. Course Information: Same as INST 283. Recommended background: POLS 184 or INST 184.

POLS 284.name: International Security
POLS 284.prerequisites: POLS 184 or INST 184 or consent of the instructor.
POLS 284.description: International conflict and cooperation, including war, nationalism, global inequality, and the environment. Course Information: Same as INST 284. Prerequisite(s): POLS 184 or INST 184 or consent of the instructor.

POLS 286.name: The United Nations and Other International Organizations
POLS 286.description: The development, structure, functioning, and impact of the United Nations and other international organizations. An assessment of their contributions and limitations. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 184 or INST 184.

POLS 287.name: International Law
POLS 287.description: The nature, scope, and limits of international law in the contemporary world. Examines three dimensions of international law: doctrine, practice, and jurisprudence (case-law). Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 184 or INST 184.

POLS 290.name: History of Political Thought I
POLS 290.description: Western political theorists from ancient Greece and Rome through the end of the classical era, including Thucydides, Plato, Aristotle, Cicero, and Augustine. Course Information: Same as CL 290. Recommended background: POLS 120.

POLS 291.name: History of Political Thought II
POLS 291.description: Western political theorists from the seventeenth century through modern times, including Machiavelli, Hobbes, Locke, Marx, Mill and Nietzsche. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 120.

POLS 295.name: Introduction to Marxism
POLS 295.description: Examination and evaluation of the basic theories of Marx and Engels to determine their contribution to the understanding of contemporary politics.

POLS 296.name: Fascism and Dictatorship in Southern Europe and the Mediterranean
POLS 296.description: Establishment of fascist and authoritarian regimes in 20th-century Spain, Italy and Greece. Fascist ideology, leadership cult, mass politics, violence and propaganda, uses of antiquity, resistance and consent, legacy and memory of fascism. Course Information: Same as HIST 296 and GKM 296. Taught in English. Prerequisite(s): ENGL 161. UIC GE Past course.

POLS 297.name: American Political Theories
POLS 297.description: American political theorists from the colonial period to the present, including Paine, Madison, Hamilton, Thoreau, Calhoun, Sumner, DuBois, and Dewey. Course Information: Recommended background: POLS 120.

POLS 300.name: Advanced Topics in Political Science
POLS 300.prerequisites: Junior or senior standing or consent of the instructor.
POLS 300.description: Selected problems in politics. Course content and format will vary to adapt to the changing political scene. Course Information: May be repeated to a maximum of 6 hours. Students may register in more than one section per term. Prerequisite(s): Junior or senior standing or consent of the instructor.

POLS 301.name: Internship in Political Science
POLS 301.prerequisites: Junior or senior standing; at least 12 credit hours in political science, including courses relevant to field experience; GPA of 3.00 in political science; and consent of instructor. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture-Discussion and one Practice.
POLS 301.description: Provides students an opportunity to apply theoretical knowledge in “real life” political settings through an internship at governmental agencies, political parties, interest groups, civic associations, international agencies, or law offices. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): Junior or senior standing; at least 12 credit hours in political science, including courses relevant to field experience; GPA of 3.00 in political science; and consent of instructor. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture-Discussion and one Practice.

POLS 302.name: Great Cities Internship
POLS 302.prerequisites: Junior or senior standing and grade point average of 3.00, or consent of the instructor. Class Schedule Information:  To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture-Discussion and one Practice.
POLS 302.description: Provides students an opportunity to apply theoretical knowledge and conduct research in metropolitan organizations through field placements and seminars. Course Information: Same as UPP 302. Prerequisite(s): Junior or senior standing and grade point average of 3.00, or consent of the instructor. Class Schedule Information:  To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture-Discussion and one Practice.

POLS 303.name: Supervised Readings and Research
POLS 303.prerequisites: Consent of the instructor. Class Schedule Information: This course counts toward the limited number of independent study hours accepted toward the degree and the major.
POLS 303.description: Supervised readings and research in political science. Course Information: May be repeated to a maximum of 6 hours. Students may register in more than one section per term. Prerequisite(s): Consent of the instructor. Class Schedule Information: This course counts toward the limited number of independent study hours accepted toward the degree and the major.

POLS 305.name: Honors Course
POLS 305.prerequisites: Open only to seniors. Requires approval of the department, a 3.25 University grade point average, and a 3.50 political science grade point average. Class Schedule Information: This course counts toward the limited number of independent study hours accepted toward the degree and the major.
POLS 305.description: Independent reading and research for students seeking departmental distinction. Course Information: May be repeated to a maximum of 6 hours. Prerequisite(s): Open only to seniors. Requires approval of the department, a 3.25 University grade point average, and a 3.50 political science grade point average. Class Schedule Information: This course counts toward the limited number of independent study hours accepted toward the degree and the major.

POLS 307.name: Political Psychology
POLS 307.prerequisites: One course in political science, psychology, or sociology.
POLS 307.description: Analysis of mass and elite political behavior from the perspectives of several psychological approaches including psychoanalytic, behavioral, humanistic and social psychology theories. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): One course in political science, psychology, or sociology.

POLS 309.name: Topics in Political Behavior
POLS 309.description: Intensive analysis of topics in political behavior. Possible topics include: elections and campaigns, political culture. Course Information: May be repeated to a maximum of 6 hours. Recommended background: POLS 206.

POLS 310.name: Governing the Megalopolis
POLS 310.prerequisites: POLS 101 or POLS 200 or the equivalent or consent of the instructor.
POLS 310.description: Examination of political/governmental issues in massive metropolitan areas, utilizing the six-county greater Chicago area for example. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): POLS 101 or POLS 200 or the equivalent or consent of the instructor.

POLS 311.name: Black Politics in the United States
POLS 311.prerequisites: Three courses in political science, history or sociology, or consent of the instructor.
POLS 311.description: Historical analysis of Black electoral politics in the U.S., including traditional political party participation and movement politics. Course Information: Same as BLST 306. Prerequisite(s): Three courses in political science, history or sociology, or consent of the instructor.

POLS 312.name: Topics in Local Politics
POLS 312.description: Intensive examination of selected problems of local politics. Actual research involvement. Topics vary. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): POLS 101 and POLS 210.

POLS 329.name: Seminar on American Politics
POLS 329.prerequisites: POLS 101 and POLS 200; or consent of the instructor. Students seeking to register with instructor approval should contact the academic advisor for Political Science, in addition to securing permission to enroll from the course instructor.
POLS 329.description: Advanced seminar on special topics in American politics. Content varies. Course Information: May be repeated to a maximum of 6 hours. Prerequisite(s): POLS 101 and POLS 200; or consent of the instructor. Students seeking to register with instructor approval should contact the academic advisor for Political Science, in addition to securing permission to enroll from the course instructor.

POLS 342.name: European Union: Politics and Institutions
POLS 342.description: An overview of the historical development of the European Union, with a focus on the functioning of its political institutions and policy-making process. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): Recommended background: POLS 130 or INST 130.

POLS 348.name: Seminar: Political Problems of Developing Societies
POLS 348.prerequisites: POLS 200 and POLS 130; or consent of the instructor.
POLS 348.description: Selected aspects of the politics and countries of Asia, Africa and Latin America. Course Information: Same as LALS 348. Prerequisite(s): POLS 200 and POLS 130; or consent of the instructor.

POLS 349.name: Topics in Comparative Politics
POLS 349.prerequisites: POLS 130 or INST 130; and POLS 200; or consent of the instructor. Students seeking to register with instructor approval should contact the academic advisor for Political Science, in addition to securing permission to enroll from the course instructor.
POLS 349.description: Selected problems in comparative politics. Course Information: Same as INST 349. May be repeated to a maximum of 6 hours if topics vary. Prerequisite(s): POLS 130 or INST 130; and POLS 200; or consent of the instructor. Students seeking to register with instructor approval should contact the academic advisor for Political Science, in addition to securing permission to enroll from the course instructor.

POLS 353.name: Constitutional Law
POLS 353.prerequisites: POLS 101 or consent of the instructor.
POLS 353.description: Selected constitutional provisions and principles as they developed through Supreme Court interpretation. Major attention given to powers and practices of, and interactions among governmental institutions. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): POLS 101 or consent of the instructor.

POLS 354.name: The Constitution and Civil Liberties
POLS 354.prerequisites: POLS 101 or consent of the instructor.
POLS 354.description: Civil rights, including religion, speech, assembly, press, and rights of the accused. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): POLS 101 or consent of the instructor.

POLS 356.name: Constitutional Law:  Women, Gender and Privacy
POLS 356.prerequisites: Grade of C or better in POLS 101 or grade of C or better in POLS 112 or grade of C or better in BLST 100 or grade of C or better in BLST 103 or grade of C or better in GWS 101; or consent of the instructor.
POLS 356.description: A multidisciplinary examination of U.S. constitutional law and politics in shaping issues of gender, privacy, race, and sexual orientation; including reproduction, labor, sexual harassment, political participation, and women and crime. Course Information: Same as BLST 356, and GWS 356. Prerequisite(s): Grade of C or better in POLS 101 or grade of C or better in POLS 112 or grade of C or better in BLST 100 or grade of C or better in BLST 103 or grade of C or better in GWS 101; or consent of the instructor.

POLS 358.name: Constitutional Law: African-American Legal History
POLS 358.description: Survey of the African-American constitutional experience from the 1600s to the present, focusing on landmark decisions of the United States Supreme Court. Course Information: Same as BLST 358.

POLS 359.name: Topics in Public Law
POLS 359.prerequisites: POLS 258 or POLS 353 or POLS 354 or POLS 356 or POLS 358 or consent of the instructor.
POLS 359.description: Selected problems arising in public law and judicial arenas. Course Information: May be repeated to a maximum of 6 hours if topics vary. Prerequisite(s): POLS 258 or POLS 353 or POLS 354 or POLS 356 or POLS 358 or consent of the instructor.

POLS 384.name: International Relations Theory
POLS 384.prerequisites: POLS 283 or POLS 284 or consent of the instructor.
POLS 384.description: Philosophical foundations of international relations, including assumptions of anarchy, rationality, power and the state. Applications to security and political economy. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): POLS 283 or POLS 284 or consent of the instructor.

POLS 389.name: Seminar: Topics in International Relations
POLS 389.prerequisites: POLS 184 or INST 184; and POLS 200; or consent of the instructor . Students seeking to register with instructor approval should contact the academic advisor for Political Science, in addition to securing permission to enroll from the course instructor.
POLS 389.description: Selected topics in international relations. Topics may vary and may cover global military, economic, cultural, ecological or methodological issues. Course Information: May be repeated to a maximum of 6 hours if topics vary. Prerequisite(s): POLS 184 or INST 184; and POLS 200; or consent of the instructor . Students seeking to register with instructor approval should contact the academic advisor for Political Science, in addition to securing permission to enroll from the course instructor.

POLS 399.name: Seminar in Political Theory
POLS 399.prerequisites: POLS 120 and POLS 200; or consent of the instructor. Students seeking to register with instructor approval should contact the academic advisor for Political Science, in addition to securing permission to enroll from the course instructor.
POLS 399.description: Selected topics and problems in political theory. Course Information: May be repeated to a maximum of 6 hours. Prerequisite(s): POLS 120 and POLS 200; or consent of the instructor. Students seeking to register with instructor approval should contact the academic advisor for Political Science, in addition to securing permission to enroll from the course instructor.

POLS 401.name: Data Analysis I
POLS 401.prerequisites: POLS 200 and POLS 201; or graduate standing.
POLS 401.description: Statistical inference for the social sciences. Emphasis on univariate and bivariate statistics. Course Information: Same as PPA 401. 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. Prerequisite(s): POLS 200 and POLS 201; or graduate standing.

POLS 402.name: Political Science Data Collection, Wrangling and Visualization
POLS 402.prerequisites: Grade of B or better in POLS 200 and Grade of B or better in POLS 201; or graduate standing; or consent of the instructor. Undergraduate students seeking to enroll in this course should have experience with data entry.
POLS 402.description: Collecting original data using tools typical for Political Science, including web scrapping, data from APIs, Qualtrics, and content analysis of documents. Cleaning data using Excel and R. Wrangling and transforming data. Data visualization. Course Information: 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. Prerequisite(s): Grade of B or better in POLS 200 and Grade of B or better in POLS 201; or graduate standing; or consent of the instructor. Undergraduate students seeking to enroll in this course should have experience with data entry.

POLS 405.name: The Problem of Justice
POLS 405.prerequisites: CLJ 101, plus two 200-level courses in CLJ or two 200-level courses in POLS.
POLS 405.description: Pre-modern, modern and non-western views of justice and their practical utility in analyzing legislative, executive, and judicial programs for enhancing or restricting justice. Course Information: Same as CLJ 405. 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. Prerequisite(s): CLJ 101, plus two 200-level courses in CLJ or two 200-level courses in POLS.

POLS 451.name: Law and Public Policy
POLS 451.description: The role of law and legal institutions in the development and implementation of public policies. Course Information: 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. Prerequisite(s): Consent of the instructor.

POLS 459.name: Advanced Topics in Urban Politics
POLS 459.prerequisites: POLS 210; or graduate standing; or consent of the instructor.
POLS 459.description: Seminar exploring urban politics theory through readings, discussion, and original research. Course Information: 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. May be repeated if topics vary. Prerequisite(s): POLS 210; or graduate standing; or consent of the instructor.

POLS 465.name: Topics in Sociology of Politics
POLS 465.prerequisites: 6 hours of upper-division sociology or consent of the instructor.
POLS 465.description: Intensive examination of a specialized topic announced when the class is scheduled. Course Information: Same as SOC 465. 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. May be repeated to a maximum of 12 hours. Students may register in more than one section per term. Prerequisite(s): 6 hours of upper-division sociology or consent of the instructor.

POLS 466.name: Criminal Governance in Latin America
POLS 466.description: Emergence of criminal politics in Latin America. Engagement of politicians, organized crime actors, and state agents in the pursuit goals (economic, political, personal). Root sources, logic and consequences of this new, prevalent order. Course Information: Same as LALS 466. 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours.

POLS 467.name: Public Opinion and Political Communication
POLS 467.prerequisites: POLS 200 or the equivalent or consent of the instructor.
POLS 467.description: Nature of public opinion and political communication systems. Patterns of opinion distribution and its measurement. Forces shaping public opinion and its impact on public policy. Course Information: Same as COMM 467. 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. Prerequisite(s): POLS 200 or the equivalent or consent of the instructor.

POLS 469.name: Advanced Topics in American Politics
POLS 469.prerequisites: Grade of B or better in POLS 101 and Grade of B or better in POLS 329; or graduate standing; or consent of the instructor.
POLS 469.description: Seminar exploring American politics theory through readings, discussion, and original research. Course Information: 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. May be repeated if  topics vary. Prerequisite(s): Grade of B or better in POLS 101 and Grade of B or better in POLS 329; or graduate standing; or consent of the instructor.

POLS 478.name: Advanced Topics in International Relations
POLS 478.prerequisites: Grade of B or better in POLS 184; and Grade of B or better in POLS 281 or Grade of B or better in POLS 283 or Grade of B or better in POLS 284; or graduate standing; or consent of the instructor.
POLS 478.description: Seminar exploring international relations theory through readings, discussion, and original research. Course Information: 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. May be repeated if topics vary. Prerequisite(s): Grade of B or better in POLS 184; and Grade of B or better in POLS 281 or Grade of B or better in POLS 283 or Grade of B or better in POLS 284; or graduate standing; or consent of the instructor.

POLS 479.name: Advanced Topics in Comparative Politics
POLS 479.prerequisites: Grade of B or better in POLS 130 and Grade of B or better in POLS 349; or graduate standing; or consent of the instructor.
POLS 479.description: Seminar exploring comparative politics theory through readings, discussion, and original research. Course Information: 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. May be repeated if topics vary. Prerequisite(s): Grade of B or better in POLS 130 and Grade of B or better in POLS 349; or graduate standing; or consent of the instructor.

POLS 482.name: Theories of Democracy and Representation
POLS 482.prerequisites: Graduate standing or consent of the instructor.
POLS 482.description: Democracy as a procedure of government and value commitments associated with this form of government. Special attention paid to classical and modern democracies. Course Information: 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing or consent of the instructor.

POLS 485.name: Gender and Politics
POLS 485.prerequisites: One 200-level course in political theory; or consent of the instructor.
POLS 485.description: Impact of gender on basic categories of western political thought. Distinctions between reason and emotion, public and private, among others, examined from feminist perspective. Course Information: Same as GWS 485. 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. Prerequisite(s): One 200-level course in political theory; or consent of the instructor.

POLS 489.name: Advanced Topics in Political Theory
POLS 489.prerequisites: Grade of B or better in POLS 290 or Grade of B or better in POLS 291; or graduate standing; or consent of the instructor.
POLS 489.description: Seminar exploring political theory through reading, discussion, and original research. Course Information: 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. May be repeated if topics vary. Prerequisite(s): Grade of B or better in POLS 290 or Grade of B or better in POLS 291; or graduate standing; or consent of the instructor.

POLS 494.name: Topics in Political Science
POLS 494.prerequisites: POLS 200; or consent of the instructor.
POLS 494.description: Selected Topics in Political Science. Topics vary and may cover American politics, law, urban and global politics, cultural, ecological or methodological issues. Course Information: May be repeated up to 1 time(s). Prerequisite(s): POLS 200; or consent of the instructor.

POLS 497.name: Directed Readings in Political Science
POLS 497.prerequisites: Graduate standing and consent of the instructor.
POLS 497.description: Intensive readings on a topic not covered in regular curriculum. Course Information: May be repeated with approval. Approval of the graduate director required to repeat course. Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing and consent of the instructor.

POLS 498.name: Independent Research in Political Science
POLS 498.prerequisites: Graduate standing and consent of the instructor.
POLS 498.description: Research on special problems not included in course offerings. May not duplicate work done in POLS 598 or POLS 599. Course Information: May be repeated with approval. Approval of the graduate director required to repeat course.  Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing and consent of the instructor.

PORT 201.name: Portuguese Blended: Brazilian Popular Culture I
PORT 201.prerequisites: PORT 240; and sophomore standing or above; or approval of the department. Recommended background: Two semesters of basic Portuguese.
PORT 201.description: Third-semester Portuguese language course that focuses on Brazilian popular culture. This is a hybrid course that combines online and face-to-face activities.Course Information: Extensive computer use required. Prerequisite(s): PORT 240; and sophomore standing or above; or approval of the department. Recommended background: Two semesters of basic Portuguese.

PORT 240.name: Rapid Portuguese for Romance Language Speakers
PORT 240.prerequisites: SPAN 202 or ITAL 201 or FR 232; or consent of the instructor.
PORT 240.description: Characteristics of Portuguese grammar from the perspective of Romance Language Speakers, with a linguistic communicative approach to develop the ability of students to understand, speak, read and write in intermediate to advanced level Portuguese. Course Information: This is a blended-online course and classroom course. Use of computer and internet access is required. A high speed connection is strongly suggested. Prerequisite(s): SPAN 202 or ITAL 201 or FR 232; or consent of the instructor.

PSCH 100.name: Introduction to Psychology
PSCH 100.description: Survey of basic concepts of contemporary psychology. Introduction to the nervous system, perception, motivation, learning and memory, social behavior, personality, developmental and clinical psychology. Course Information: Students under 18 years of age need parental consent to participate in research experiments that are part of the course. Instructions for obtaining parental consent will be provided during class early in the semester. Class Schedule Information:  To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Discussion/Recitation and one Lecture. IAI GECC Soc/Behav Sci course, and UIC GE Individual and Society course.

PSCH 184.name: The Basics of Neuroscience
PSCH 184.description: Introduction to the scientific study of the brain and behavior. Overview of neuroscience as an integrative discipline. Course Information: Same as PHIL 184 and BIOS 184. Satisfactory/Unsatisfactory grading only.

PSCH 210.name: Theories of Personality
PSCH 210.prerequisites: PSCH 100. UIC GE Individual and Society course.
PSCH 210.description: Survey of major theoretical approaches to the study of personality and the evidential basis underlying each approach. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 100. UIC GE Individual and Society course.

PSCH 231.name: Community Psychology
PSCH 231.prerequisites: PSCH 100. UIC GE Individual and Society course.
PSCH 231.description: Psychological principles, research and interventions concerning community settings; community human services, primary prevention, consultation, advocacy, social ecology, organizational change, and citizen participation. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 100. UIC GE Individual and Society course.

PSCH 242.name: Introduction to Research in Psychology
PSCH 242.prerequisites: Grade of C or better in PSCH 100. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Discussion/Recitation and one Lecture.
PSCH 242.description: Techniques and problems associated with the study of behavior. Introduction to statistical inference, probability distributions, sampling, and hypothesis testing (e.g., t-tests). Course Information: Consult the class schedule for details on the method of instruction. Courses taught in online or hybrid formats involve heavy computer usage; computer and internet access are required. Labs will require use of statistical software. Prerequisite(s): Grade of C or better in PSCH 100. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Discussion/Recitation and one Lecture.

PSCH 262.name: Behavioral Neuroscience
PSCH 262.description: Research and theories concerning the physiological bases of behavior. Understanding of basic brain organization with emphasis on neural substrates of learning, motivation and perception. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 100.

PSCH 270.name: Introduction to Psychological and Behavioral Disorders
PSCH 270.prerequisites: PSCH 100. IAI Major PSY course, and UIC GE Individual and Society course.
PSCH 270.description: A survey course covering the assessment, description, causes, and treatments of many psychological disorders, including depression, anxiety disorders, psychosis, sexual dysfunction, and personality disorders. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 100. IAI Major PSY course, and UIC GE Individual and Society course.

PSCH 271.name: Resilience: The Psychology of Positive Adaptation
PSCH 271.prerequisites: PSCH 100. UIC GE Individual and Society course.
PSCH 271.description: An overview of resilience research, definitions, correlates, and causes of successful adaptation in response to stress and adversity. Application on scientifically-validated strategies for improving coping skills and mental health. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 100. UIC GE Individual and Society course.

PSCH 272.name: Psychology of Climate Change
PSCH 272.description: Research and theories concerning human behavioral causes of climate change and evidence-based approaches toward addressing mental health impacts. Emphasis on strategies for individual and systemic behavior change. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 100.

PSCH 303.name: Writing in Psychology
PSCH 303.prerequisites: PSCH 242 and ENGL 161 with a minimum grade of C; MATH 108 or MATH 118 (or the equivalent) with a minimum grade of C or MATH 090; or consent of the instructor. For psychology majors only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Discussion/Recitation and one Lecture.
PSCH 303.description: Teaches students the fundamentals of scientific writing including literature reviews, research reports and book reviews. Students will write a minimum of three papers dealing with psychological topics. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 242 and ENGL 161 with a minimum grade of C; MATH 108 or MATH 118 (or the equivalent) with a minimum grade of C or MATH 090; or consent of the instructor. For psychology majors only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Discussion/Recitation and one Lecture.

PSCH 312.name: Social Psychology
PSCH 312.prerequisites: Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.
PSCH 312.description: Survey of theory and research in social psychology, emphasizing experimental investigations of attitudes and social cognition, and interpersonal relations and group processes. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.

PSCH 313.name: Laboratory in Social Psychology
PSCH 313.prerequisites: PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 312. For Psychology majors only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must be enrolled in one Lecture/Discussion and one Laboratory.
PSCH 313.description: Conduct laboratory and field experiments in social psychology on problems in attitudes and social cognition, and interpersonal relations and group processes. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 312. For Psychology majors only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must be enrolled in one Lecture/Discussion and one Laboratory.

PSCH 315.name: Psychology of Women and Gender
PSCH 315.prerequisites: Grade of C or better in PSCH 242 or consent of the instructor.
PSCH 315.description: Critical examination of research on women and gender across the life span, including psychological aspects of reproduction, and the way that gender shapes cognition, sexuality, family, friendship, and work experiences. Course Information: Same as GWS 315. Prerequisite(s): Grade of C or better in PSCH 242 or consent of the instructor.

PSCH 320.name: Developmental Psychology
PSCH 320.prerequisites: Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.
PSCH 320.description: Analysis of research and theory concerning social, cognitive, and biopsychological aspects of human development. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.

PSCH 321.name: Laboratory in Developmental Psychology
PSCH 321.prerequisites: PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 320. For Psychology majors only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture/Discussion and one Laboratory.
PSCH 321.description: Survey of dominant research strategies in contemporary developmental psychology. Laboratory experience in developmental research. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 320. For Psychology majors only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture/Discussion and one Laboratory.

PSCH 324.name: Cultural Psychology
PSCH 324.prerequisites: PSCH 100 and Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.
PSCH 324.description: A survey of theory, methods, and research in culture and psychology. Examination of  how culture is defined and studied and how it affects development, socialization, personality, interpersonal relations, and mental health. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 100 and Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.

PSCH 331.name: Laboratory in Community and Prevention Research
PSCH 331.prerequisites: PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 231. For Psychology majors only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture/Discussion and one Laboratory.
PSCH 331.description: Examines how researchers conceptualize, design, implement, and evaluate school and community programs to enhance competence, promote empowerment, and prevent behavioral problems. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 231. For Psychology majors only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture/Discussion and one Laboratory.

PSCH 333.name: Laboratory in Clinical Psychology
PSCH 333.prerequisites: PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 210 or PSCH 270. For Psychology majors only. Class Schedule: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture-Discussion and one Laboratory.
PSCH 333.description: Laboratory practicum in self-change processes, empirically validated treatments, experimental psychopathology, and clinical assessment development. Course Information: Extensive computer use required. Prerequisite(s): PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 210 or PSCH 270. For Psychology majors only. Class Schedule: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture-Discussion and one Laboratory.

PSCH 340.name: Psychological Testing
PSCH 340.prerequisites: Grade of C or better in PSCH 242 and Grade of C or better in PSCH 270.
PSCH 340.description: Introduction to principles of psychological assessment, with an overview of representative techniques. Particular emphasis is placed on objective tests. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): Grade of C or better in PSCH 242 and Grade of C or better in PSCH 270.

PSCH 343.name: Statistical Methods in Behavioral Science
PSCH 343.prerequisites: PSCH 242 and ENGL 161 with a minimum grade of C; MATH 105 or MATH 108 (or the equivalent) with a minimum grade of C or satisfactory completion of Math 090. For psychology majors or students in the Neuroscience degree program only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Discussion/Recitation and one Lecture.
PSCH 343.description: Introduction to statistical inference, probability distributions, sampling, hypothesis testing, correlation and analysis of variance. Course Information: Credit is not given for PSCH 343 if the student has credit for IDS 371. Consult the class schedule for details on method of instruction for this course. Course taught in an online or hybrid format involves heavy computer usage; computer and internet access are required. Prerequisite(s): PSCH 242 and ENGL 161 with a minimum grade of C; MATH 105 or MATH 108 (or the equivalent) with a minimum grade of C or satisfactory completion of Math 090. For psychology majors or students in the Neuroscience degree program only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Discussion/Recitation and one Lecture.

PSCH 350.name: Sensation and Perception
PSCH 350.prerequisites: Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.
PSCH 350.description: Survey of theories and empirical findings in the study of sensation and perception. Emphasis on human vision and audition as information processing systems. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.

PSCH 351.name: Laboratory in Perception
PSCH 351.prerequisites: PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 350. For psychology majors or students in the neuroscience degree program only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture-Discussion and one Laboratory.
PSCH 351.description: Laboratory practicum in sensation and perception. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 350. For psychology majors or students in the neuroscience degree program only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture-Discussion and one Laboratory.

PSCH 352.name: Cognition and Memory
PSCH 352.prerequisites: Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.
PSCH 352.description: Survey of experimental findings in human learning, memory, attention, knowledge representation, problem solving, conceptual behavior, and psycholinguistics. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.

PSCH 353.name: Laboratory in Cognition and Memory
PSCH 353.prerequisites: PSCH 343; and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 352 or credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 320. For psychology majors or students in the neuroscience degree program only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture/Discussion and one Laboratory.
PSCH 353.description: Laboratory practicum in memory, psycholinguistics, problem solving, and reasoning. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 343; and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 352 or credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 320. For psychology majors or students in the neuroscience degree program only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture/Discussion and one Laboratory.

PSCH 354.name: Knowledge Acquisition
PSCH 354.prerequisites: Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.
PSCH 354.description: Introduction to belief formation, conceptual change, cognitive development, discovery and invention, schema abstraction, skill acquisition and other cognitive change processes. Research and practical applications. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.

PSCH 360.name: Learning and Conditioning
PSCH 360.prerequisites: Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.
PSCH 360.description: Principles of learning; emphasis on parameters of acquisition, extinction and retention of classical and instrumental conditioning and stimulus control of reflexive and voluntary behavior. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.

PSCH 361.name: Laboratory in Learning and Conditioning
PSCH 361.prerequisites: PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 360. For psychology majors or students in the neuroscience degree program only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture/Discussion and one Laboratory.
PSCH 361.description: Laboratory practicum in conditioning and simple learning using animal subjects. Emphasis on operant conditioning. Course Information: Animals used in instruction. Prerequisite(s): PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 360. For psychology majors or students in the neuroscience degree program only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture/Discussion and one Laboratory.

PSCH 363.name: Laboratory in Behavioral Neuroscience
PSCH 363.prerequisites: PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 262 or BIOS 286. For psychology majors or students in the neuroscience degree program only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lacture/Discussion and one Laboratory.
PSCH 363.description: Laboratory practicum and demonstrations of research techniques used to study the physiological bases of behavior. Course Information: Animals used in instruction. Prerequisite(s): PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 262 or BIOS 286. For psychology majors or students in the neuroscience degree program only. Class Schedule Information: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lacture/Discussion and one Laboratory.

PSCH 366.name: Cognitive Neuroscience
PSCH 366.prerequisites: Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.
PSCH 366.description: Survey of neural basis of cognitive processes; perceptual and motor systems, attention, memory, symbolic representation, executive control of behavior. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.

PSCH 367.name: Laboratory in Cognitive Neuroscience
PSCH 367.prerequisites: PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 350 or PSCH 366. For psychology majors or students in the neuroscience degree program only. Class Schedule: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture and one Laboratory.
PSCH 367.description: Laboratory practicum in attention, memory, decision-making and symbolic cognitive processes. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 343 and credit or concurrent registration in PSCH 350 or PSCH 366. For psychology majors or students in the neuroscience degree program only. Class Schedule: To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Lecture and one Laboratory.

PSCH 380.name: Positive Psychology: The Science of Well-Being
PSCH 380.prerequisites: PSCH 210 or PSCH 231 or PSCH 312; and Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.
PSCH 380.description: An introduction to the study of topics related to happiness and the positive aspects human experience and overall well-being. Basic areas of research in positive psychology and the methods that researchers use to study happiness. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 210 or PSCH 231 or PSCH 312; and Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.

PSCH 381.name: Psychology of Interviewing
PSCH 381.prerequisites: PSCH 210 or PSCH 231 or PSCH 270 or PSCH 271 or PSCH 312; and Grade of C or better in PSCH 242. For psychology majors only.
PSCH 381.description: The goals of the course are to teach theory, research and practice of interviewing. Students will develop skills for interviewing individuals.  Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 210 or PSCH 231 or PSCH 270 or PSCH 271 or PSCH 312; and Grade of C or better in PSCH 242. For psychology majors only.

PSCH 382.name: Psychological Interventions
PSCH 382.prerequisites: PSCH 210 or PSCH 231 or PSCH 312; and PSCH 270 and a grade of C or better in PSCH 242. For Psychology majors only.
PSCH 382.description: Application of psychological theories and principles used in psychotherapy and behavior change. Emphasis will be on models for assessment of change, the evaluation of psychotherapy, and selecting appropriate interventions. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 210 or PSCH 231 or PSCH 312; and PSCH 270 and a grade of C or better in PSCH 242. For Psychology majors only.

PSCH 384.name: Psychology of Groups
PSCH 384.prerequisites: PSCH 210 or PSCH 231 or PSCH 312; and Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.
PSCH 384.description: Application of psychological theory and research to group functioning and change. Survey of intervention methods emphasizing the development of competence in group participation and leadership. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 210 or PSCH 231 or PSCH 312; and Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.

PSCH 385.name: Field Work in Applied Psychology
PSCH 385.prerequisites: PSCH 340 and PSCH 343; and PSCH 380 or PSCH 381 or PSCH 382 or PSCH 384. For applied psychology majors only. Class Schedule Information:  To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Discussion/Recitation and one Practice.
PSCH 385.description: Supervised practicum as a paraprofessional worker for a minimum equivalent of one day (8 hours) per week in a mental health, developmental disabilities, or industrial organizational setting. Course Information:  6 hours. Prerequisite(s): PSCH 340 and PSCH 343; and PSCH 380 or PSCH 381 or PSCH 382 or PSCH 384. For applied psychology majors only. Class Schedule Information:  To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Discussion/Recitation and one Practice.

PSCH 386.name: Crisis Counseling Techniques I
PSCH 386.prerequisites: Grade of C or better in PSCH 242; and PSCH 210 or PSCH 231 or PSCH 270; and consent of the instructor.
PSCH 386.description: Application of psychological theories and principles used for telephone crisis counseling, crisis intervention, and referral.  Emphasis will be on models for interviewing and assessment and appropriate intervention in crisis situations. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): Grade of C or better in PSCH 242; and PSCH 210 or PSCH 231 or PSCH 270; and consent of the instructor.

PSCH 387.name: Crisis Counseling Techniques II
PSCH 387.prerequisites: PSCH 386; and consent of the instructor.
PSCH 387.description: Students will apply skills learned in PSCH 386 as they run weekly support groups providing both peer-to-peer counseling per week in a group format as well as individual peer-to-peer crisis counseling with group members. Course Information: Satisfactory/Unsatisfactory grading only. Field work required. Prerequisite(s): PSCH 386; and consent of the instructor.

PSCH 394.name: Special Topics in Psychology
PSCH 394.prerequisites: Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.
PSCH 394.description: Lectures devoted to an announced topic. Course Information: May be repeated to a maximum of 6 hours. Students may register in more than one section per term. Prerequisite(s): Grade of C or better in PSCH 242.

PSCH 396.name: Directed Research
PSCH 396.prerequisites: PSCH 242 and consent of the instructor. Must consult intructor for permission to enroll.
PSCH 396.description: Participation in ongoing research in psychology under the direction of a faculty member. A final report describing the research and its theory is required. Course Information: Satisfactory/Unsatisfactory grading only. May be repeated. Students may register in more than one section per term. A combined maximum of 8 hours of credit in PSCH 387, PSCH 396, PSCH 397, and PSCH 399 may be applied toward the degree. Either PSCH 396 or an additional laboratory course is required for High Distinction in Psychology. Prerequisite(s): PSCH 242 and consent of the instructor. Must consult intructor for permission to enroll.

PSCH 397.name: Readings in Psychology
PSCH 397.prerequisites: PSCH 343 and consent of the instructor. Must consult instructor for permission to enroll.
PSCH 397.description: Bibliographic research on a special topic under the direction of a faculty member. Paper is required for course credit. Course Information: May be repeated. Students may register in more than one section per term. A combined maximum of 8 hours of credit in PSCH 387, PSCH 396, PSCH 397, and PSCH 399 may be applied toward the degree. Prerequisite(s): PSCH 343 and consent of the instructor. Must consult instructor for permission to enroll.

PSCH 399.name: Independent Research
PSCH 399.prerequisites: PSCH 343; and consent of the instructor and the director of undergraduate studies.
PSCH 399.description: Individual research on a special topic under the direction of a faculty member. A paper is required for course credit. Course Information: May be repeated. Students may register in more than one section per term. A combined maximum of 8 hours of credit in PSCH 387, PSCH 396, PSCH 397, and PSCH 399 may be applied toward the degree.  Required for earning Highest Distinction in Psychology. Prerequisite(s): PSCH 343; and consent of the instructor and the director of undergraduate studies.

PSCH 411.name: The Psychology of Racism
PSCH 411.prerequisites: PSCH 242, graduate standing, or consent of Instructor.
PSCH 411.description: Within our country’s context of racism, you might ask yourself: What is my role in resisting racism? How might I (as a Person of Color) heal from racism and work in solidarity with other People of Color to dismantle White supremacy? Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 242, graduate standing, or consent of Instructor.

PSCH 415.name: Social Bases of Health Behavior
PSCH 415.prerequisites: PSCH 270 and consent of the instructor, or graduate standing.
PSCH 415.description: Psychological theory and research concerning the coronary-prone personality, pain management, controlling adherence to medical regimens, biofeedback, smoking, and weight control. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 270 and consent of the instructor, or graduate standing.

PSCH 417.name: Psychology and Law
PSCH 417.description: Application of psychological theories to the development, operation and effects of law; evaluation of different and similar approaches of law and psychology. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): PSCH 312 or consent of instructor.

PSCH 420.name: Advanced Social Development of Children in an Urban Context
PSCH 420.description: Examines some general principles of children’s (3 -14 years old) social development and socialization and the applicability of these principles for urban-dwelling children. Course Information: Same as EPSY 420. 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours.

PSCH 422.name: Advanced Developmental Psychology and Educational Processes
PSCH 422.prerequisites: PSCH 100 and any one from EPSY 210, PSCH 259, PSCH 320 and consent of the instructor; or graduate standing.
PSCH 422.description: Focuses on cognitive and social development from birth to early adolescence. Examines relations between development, learning, and educational processes. Course Information: Same as ED 422. Prerequisite(s):PSCH 100 and any one from EPSY 210, PSCH 259, PSCH 320 and consent of the instructor; or graduate standing.

PSCH 423.name: Characteristics of Early Adolescence
PSCH 423.prerequisites: Admission to a program in psychology or education; or approval of the College of Education or consent of the instructor, EPSY 210 or EPSY 255 or ED 421 or ED 422.
PSCH 423.description: Physiological, social, emotional and cognitive development of early adolescence. The relationship between these developmental characteristics and success in the middle grades. Course Information: Same as EPSY 446. Prerequisite(s): Admission to a program in psychology or education; or approval of the College of Education or consent of the instructor, EPSY 210 or EPSY 255 or ED 421 or ED 422.

PSCH 424.name: Social and Emotional Learning: Research, Practice, and Policy
PSCH 424.prerequisites: PSCH 343 or equivalent; or consent of the instructor. Recommended background: Experience working with children or adolescents.
PSCH 424.description: Research, theory, educational practices, and federal/state policies that promote the social, emotional, and academic competence of students who are in preschool, middle school, or high school. Course Information: Same as ED 424. 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. Prerequisite(s): PSCH 343 or equivalent; or consent of the instructor. Recommended background: Experience working with children or adolescents.

PSCH 429.name: Constructivist Approaches to Development: Piaget and Vygotsky
PSCH 429.prerequisites: EPSY 255 or EPSY 426 or ED 422; and senior standing or above; or consent of the instructor.
PSCH 429.description: Piaget's and Vygotsky's theories of development of knowledge. Empirical and logico-mathematical forms of knowledge. Thought and action. Thought and language. Course Information: Same as EPSY 429. Prerequisite(s): EPSY 255 or EPSY 426 or ED 422; and senior standing or above; or consent of the instructor.

PSCH 443.name: Advanced Statistics
PSCH 443.prerequisites: PSCH 343. Class Schedule Information:  To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Discussion/Recitation and one Lecture.
PSCH 443.description: Design and analysis of experiments: between, within factorial and mixed factorial designs and introduction to multiple regression. For students planning research careers or advanced degrees. Course Information: 3 hours. Prerequisite(s): PSCH 343. Class Schedule Information:  To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Discussion/Recitation and one Lecture.

PSCH 459.name: Cognitive Methods
PSCH 459.prerequisites: Graduate standing or consent of the instructor. Class Schedule Information:  To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Laboratory and one Lecture-Discussion.
PSCH 459.description: Hands-on training in the methods of cognitive psychology, especially computational modeling and the analysis of verbal protocols and other types of trace data. Course Information: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing or consent of the instructor. Class Schedule Information:  To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Laboratory and one Lecture-Discussion.

PSCH 483.name: Neuroanatomy
PSCH 483.prerequisites: BIOS 286 or PSCH 262 or BIOS 325 or BIOS 272; or consent of the instructor. Class Schedule Information:  To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Laboratory and one Lecture.
PSCH 483.description: Organization of the nervous system, with an emphasis on mammals. Course Information: Same as BIOS 483 and NEUS 483. Animals used in instruction. Prerequisite(s): BIOS 286 or PSCH 262 or BIOS 325 or BIOS 272; or consent of the instructor. Class Schedule Information:  To be properly registered, students must enroll in one Laboratory and one Lecture.

PSCH 484.name: Neuroscience I
PSCH 484.description: Neuroscience as an integrative discipline. Neuroanatomy of vertebrates, neural development, cellular neurobiology, action potential mechanisms, synaptic transmission and neuropharmacology.  Course Information:  Same as BIOS 484 and PHIL 484.  Prerequisite(s):  BIOS 286 or PSCH 262.

PSCH 485.name: Neuroscience II
PSCH 485.description: Integrative neuroscience, including sensory and motor systems; learning, memory, and language; pathology of nervous systems; philosophical perspectives, and modeling. Course Information:  Same as BIOS 485 and PHIL 485. Prerequisite(s): BIOS 286 or PSCH 262.

PSCH 494.name: Special Topics in Psychology
PSCH 494.prerequisites: Graduate standing or consent of the instructor.
PSCH 494.description: Advanced treatment of an announced topic. Course Information: May be repeated. Students may register in more than one section per term. Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing or consent of the instructor.

PA 401.name: Foundations of Public Service
PA 401.prerequisites: Master of Public Administration or Master of Public Policy or Master of Science in Civic Analytics program or consent of the instructor.
PA 401.description: Examines political, legal, economic and social environment of public service, internal dynamics of public organizations, relationship between public organizations and external stakeholders, and ethical and moral challenges.  Course Information: 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. Previously listed as PA 400. Prerequisite(s): Master of Public Administration or Master of Public Policy or Master of Science in Civic Analytics program or consent of the instructor.

PA 402.name: Introduction to Data Management and Analysis
PA 402.prerequisites: Junior standing or above Admission to the MPA, MPP, or MSCA program; or consent of the instructor.
PA 402.description: Topics and methods related to collecting and analyzing data by government and nonprofit organizations to make better decisions and improve operations. Course Information: 3 undergraduate hours; 4 graduate hours. Previously listed as PA 407. Prerequisite(s): Junior standing or above Admission to the MPA, MPP, or MSCA program; or consent of the instructor.

PA 403.name: Economics for Management and Policy
PA 403.prerequisites: Master of Public Administration or consent of the instructor.
PA 403.description: Basic economic tools and methods relevant to public admin and current policy: opportunity cost, supply and demand, rational choice, production costs, competition vs monopoly, and economic efficiency versus equity, market failure, and public goods. Course Information: 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. Previously listed as PA 410. Prerequisite(s):  Master of Public Administration or consent of the instructor.

PA 412.name: Addressing Public Problems with Data
PA 412.prerequisites: Admission to the Master of Public Administration, Master of Public Policy, or Master of Science in Civic Analytics program or consent of the instructor.
PA 412.description: Intended to explore best practices for utilizing data and innovative approaches to application development while ensuring commitment to the public values of access, equity, and sustainability. Course Information:  3 undergraduate hours; 4 graduate hours. Prerequisite(s): Admission to the Master of Public Administration, Master of Public Policy, or Master of Science in Civic Analytics program or consent of the instructor.

PA 421.name: Public Management
PA 421.prerequisites: Admission to the MPA program or consent of the instructor.
PA 421.description: Theories, concepts, and applications of management in public organizations. Topics include bureaucracy, public versus private organizations, leadership, motivation, and managing organization change, discretion, and networks. Course Information: Previously listed as PA 415. 3 undergraduate hours. 4 graduate hours. Prerequisite(s): Admission to the MPA program or consent of the instructor.

PA 422.name: Project Management
PA 422.description: A comprehensive review of project management practices and techniques necessary to successfully manage projects in government and non-profit organizations which is rooted largely in Project Management Institute (PMI) standards and practices. Course Information: Previously listed as PA 462.

PA 431.name: Civic Technology
PA 431.description: Application of the Internet and related technologies for public management. Topics include, Web-based service delivery, online governance, the technological divide, the use of social media, and the changing role of public managers.
